zh.lifeSummary: William De Deighton是William De Deighton和Joan De Morton的兒子，出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾朗 馬爾斯頓。他於1484年與Margaret Barker在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾朗 馬爾斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1537年在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Deighton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Durham): habitational name from any of several places in Yorkshire named Deighton, from Old English dīc ‘ditch, dike’ + tūn ‘settlement, enclosure’. See also Ditton .

variants.name: Ditton
meanings: English (Kent): habitational name from any of numerous places called Ditton, for example Ditton in Kent, Buckinghamshire, and Lancashire; Fen Ditton or Wood Ditton in Cambridgeshire; or Thames Ditton or Long Ditton in Surrey, all from Old English dīc ‘ditch, dike’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement, farmstead’.

variants.name: Dinsdale
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Over Dinsdale (North Yorkshire) or Low Dinsdale (Durham), which stand on opposite sides of the Tees river. The name arises from Old English Dīctūneshalh ‘nook, recess (Old English halh) belonging to Deighton ’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William De Deighton was born in Long Marston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom as the son of William De Deighton and Joan De Morton. He married Margaret Barker in 1484, in Long Marston, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1537, in his hometown.

ja.lifeSummary: William De Deightonはイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアロング マーストンでWilliam De DeightonとJoan De Mortonの息子として生まれた。1484年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアロング マーストンでMargaret Barkerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1537年に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William De Deighton(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 론그 마스턴에서 William De Deighton(와)과 Joan De Morton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1484년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 론그 마스턴에서 Margaret Barker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1537년 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: William De Deighton родился в местности лонг марстон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали William De Deighton и Joan De Morton. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Barker в 1484 г. в местности лонг марстон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1537 г. в его родном городе.
